_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Fix-cycle agent has no allowed_paths enforcement — drifts or reverts

steps.description: Inject scope.allowed_paths into the fix-cycle prompt (operator-readable block at the top); before/after the cycle, snapshot working-tree paths via git diff --name-only HEAD + git ls-files --others; on any out-of-scope edit set FixCycle.status = FixStatus.escalated (existing enum value) and emit a DaemonEvent of type scope_violation_escalation, without auto-reverting the agent's edits and without advancing the step. Add a daemon log line per cycle: 'fix_cycle scope: allowed=N in_scope=K out_of_scope=M'.
Per-agent review of S01: confirm prompt injection, scope-match logic, no auto-revert behaviour, escalation path is clean, log line present.

Extend tests/integration/test_fix_cycle_scope_enforcement.py (S01 already authored the AC1 reproduction test) with two new regression tests: AC3 (operator pre-edit on an out-of-scope file is preserved — cycle status remains FixStatus.completed, no violation recorded) and AC4 (in-scope edits advance normally — cycle status FixStatus.completed, no scope_violation_escalation event). Assertions reference the existing FixStatus enum, not any new string outcome.
Per-agent review of S03 tests: assertion strength (semantic, not shape), reproduction test would fail pre-fix, regression coverage of all three AC paths.
Cross-agent final review of S01..S04: all three ACs covered, no scope creep, prompt+behaviour parity, daemon log line shape matches.

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill — focus on whether the new escalation path itself produced any agent thrash, and whether the prompt-injected allowed_paths block survived all cycles.
